questions.prompt: A bond has annual default probability 2% and recovery rate 30%. What is the approximate fair credit spread over Treasury (basis points)? Use the approximation spread ≈ p · (1 − δ).
questions.explanation: Spread ≈ 0.02 × (1 − 0.30) = 0.014 = 140 bps. Real-world spreads typically exceed this to compensate for risk on top of expected loss.

questions.prompt: Which rating boundary is the most operationally important because many institutional investors are forbidden by mandate from holding bonds below it?
choices: BBB- vs BB+ (investment grade vs speculative)
questions.explanation: The investment-grade/speculative line at BBB-/BB+ is the legal threshold for many pension funds and insurance portfolios; downgrades across it force selling and widen spreads.

questions.prompt: Which of the following tend to widen credit spreads?
choices: Tighter monetary policy hurting cyclical firms
questions.explanation: Recession, lower risk appetite, and tightening that hurts borrowers all widen spreads. *Falling* firm leverage tends to *narrow* spreads (lower default risk).

questions.prompt: If high-yield spreads jumped from 250 bps to 1,000 bps during a financial crisis, what's the most accurate interpretation?
choices: Expected default losses alone widened 750 bps
Both expected default losses AND the risk premium investors demand both rose
Default probabilities fell, but recovery rates rose
questions.explanation: In crises both pieces grow: actual default rates climb and the risk premium demanded for bearing that risk climbs even faster. Separating the two is a research-active topic.

furtherReading.title: Country Garden, Evergrande Set Up Two Tense Weeks for Creditors
furtherReading.why: Country Garden's bonds collapsed from 80 cents to roughly 8 cents on the dollar in 2023 — a vivid demonstration of credit spreads exploding as default probability rises.
